name: FII (Foreign Institutional Investor) Flows — India
summary: Created 2026-05-12. **DAY 8 (May 18): ₹96 TRIGGER FIRES — USD/INR ₹96.28-96.29 RECORD LOW; FII YTD ₹2 LAKH CRORE EXIT EXCEEDS FULL-YEAR 2025 BY ~₹40K CR.** FPI ownership ~16% (15-year low, BELOW DII for first time ever). **Reuters has ranked the rupee as the worst-performing Asian currency in 2026.** Sensex/Nifty open -1.30%/-1.04% Monday on Brent $111.47 (+2.0%, StanChart $130-145 path activates), Saudi drone interception (B1), Trump-Netanyahu coordination. **The cascade is now self-reinforcing**: Brent $111 → CAD widening → FII outflow → INR ₹96 trigger fires → RBI binary (defend/capitulate/administrative). At $111 Brent, FII channel will not turn — May 14 bounce fully reversed. **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: requires (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) USD/INR ₹93 — NONE triggered, all moving wrong direction. **The RBI's weekly reserves print for week ending May 16 has NOT yet printed through Monday morning IST (lands Monday-Tuesday)**. **Tuesday May 19 Trump NSC binary**: strike order → FII channel cracks further (Nifty 21,500-22,000 test); back-off → potential FII bounce. **PRIOR (May 16): FII YTD ₹2 LAKH CRORE EXIT (EXCEEDS FULL-YEAR 2025); MAY FII RUNNING ₹14,231 CR OUTFLOW SO FAR; FPI OWNERSHIP ~16% (LOWEST IN 2 DECADES, BELOW DII FOR FIRST TIME).** **A9 RBI FOREX RESERVES — most recent data is week ending May 8 = $696.99B (+$6.3B w/w recovery from May 1 $690.69B)**; week-ending May 15 print not yet released. Yesterday's brief 'anything above $10B this week moves the sustainability question from theoretical to operational' specification remains a PENDING test. **A10 USD/INR — May 15 intraday high ₹95.9825 = 1.75 paise from breaching ₹96 Varma trigger (Day 4 of 5-7 session window)** — RBI direct intervention near ₹95.96 is the operational defence. **Sensex closed May 15 at 75,238 (-0.21%); Nifty -0.19% at 23,643.50** — Indian markets halted Thursday's bounce; late selling in metals and oil/gas; Tata Motors +5.14%, Infosys +1.92%, Tech Mahindra +1.79% (IT-pack rotation on US recession risk). Bank Nifty -0.8% / -11.9% 3M = RBI regulatory drag carries forward. **At $109 Brent, FII channel will not turn — May 14 bounce now under threat.** RBI primary-dealer trading volumes hike May 15 (annual targets for 21 bond market makers) is the liquidity-providing measure — India 10Y stayed flat-to-easing despite global yield shock. Government considering bond-tax cut for foreign investors (partial relief if announced). PRIOR (May 15): **DAY 5 (May 15): ₹2 LAKH CRORE EXITED INDIAN EQUITIES IN FIRST 5 MONTHS OF 2026 — SURPASSING ALL OF 2025'S ₹1.66 LAKH CRORE.** **FPI ownership now ~16% — lowest in 15-20 years AND BELOW DII FOR THE FIRST TIME EVER**. **$19B of that outflow since Iran war started — flow is overwhelmingly crisis-linked.** USD/INR ₹95.90 fourth straight record close; foreign safe-haven USD bid on Trump Iran ultimatum amplifies pressure. **Indian markets bounced May 14 after 4-session losing streak**: Sensex +789.74 / +1.06% to 75,398.72; Nifty +277 / +1.18% to 23,689.60 (pharma, healthcare and metals leading; Cipla, Adani Enterprises, Bharti Airtel top gainers) — but bounce is summit-pause-pricing on Trump-Xi joint statement, NOT regime reversal. **Government considering bond-tax cut for foreign investors — partial relief signal for INR; not yet announced.** **Sonal Varma's ₹96-within-5-7-sessions trigger Day 3 of window. RBI weekly reserves print due May 16 = next sustainability test.** PRIOR (May 14): Markets dossier C2 reversibility requirements unchanged: (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) USD/INR ₹93 — none triggered. **FII (Foreign Institutional Investor) net cash-equity flows into Indian listed equities are the TRANSMISSION MECHANISM** in every India-market causal chain of the cycle. Tracked via NSDL/NSE primary daily prints. Sonal Varma (Nomura): 'Don't expect any sustained pick-up in portfolio flows, as structural concerns over AI-related disruption to India's software industry remain'; recent RBI restrictions on AD forex hedging have increased FX hedge costs, deterring portfolio inflows; sees 'further scope for the rupee to depreciate.' **DAY 4 (May 14): FII DATA VIA NSDL NOT YET RELEASED FOR MAY 13 SETTLEMENT — Jan-May 2026 cumulative ~₹1.97 lakh crore (Business Standard 'Personal Finance' note; March 2026 was largest monthly outflow in 6 years at $13.6bn).** Indian markets bounced 2 consecutive sessions on Trump-Xi summit-pricing (Sensex +0.07% May 13 / +1.0% May 14 morning intraday; Nifty +0.14% / +1.1%) — bounce is summit-pause-pricing, NOT regime reversal; cumulative drawdown from May 11 record still ~-1.83%. **C7 USD/INR ₹95.74 May 14 morning (₹95.31 May 13 close = 4th straight session fresh closing record); pace of weakening MATERIALLY SLOWED from May 11-12 collapse — RBI intervention sustaining defence at ₹95.50-75 zone.** ₹96 trigger Day 2 of 5-7 not breached. **RBI weekly reserves print due ~May 16 (Friday)** = critical signal — if extends above $10bn, intervention sustainability moves from theoretical to operational concern. **Markets dossier C2 reversibility requirements unchanged: (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) USD/INR ₹93 — none triggered.** If Trump leaves Beijing May 15 without deliverable HEU framework, expect today's gains fully reverse Friday-Monday. **Watch Critical: Friday NSDL May 14 FII print — first POSITIVE day would be regime-shift signal**. PRIOR (May 13): **DAY 2 EXTENSION (May 13): FIIs CONTINUED NET SELLERS May 12 (multiple outlets confirm continuation rather than reversal of May 11 ₹8,437 cr outflow pattern); Moody's CUT India FY26 GDP 0.8PP TO 6.0% adds SOVEREIGN-RATING channel to structural FII case; Sensex closed May 12 at 74,559.24 (-1,456 pts, -1.92%) = largest 2-session drawdown of cycle; Hardeep Puri quantified OMC pain at ₹1,000 cr/day = fiscal-deficit channel quantified.** May 13 intraday relief bounce in Nifty/Sensex is technical not fundamental — cumulative drawdown -1.83% from May 11 peak. RBI reserves drawdown next print due ~May 16. FII (Foreign Institutional Investor) net cash-equity flows into Indian listed equities are the **TRANSMISSION MECHANISM** in every India-market causal chain of the cycle. Tracked via NSDL/NSE primary daily prints. **MAY 11 FII SINGLE-DAY NET SELL ₹8,437.56 cr (highest since April 24); DII +₹5,939.65 cr absorbed ~70%; YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 LAKH CR — already exceeds full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh cr by ₹40k cr (+₹14k cr in just 2 sessions May 11-12).** Drivers: Trump rejection of Iran counter-proposal + Brent $105.58 + INR breach of ₹95.50 RBI threshold. **The cascade is now self-reinforcing: weaker rupee → imported inflation → bond yields up (G-Sec 6.96-6.99%) → debt-FPI also out → more INR pressure → more FII exit.** DII absorption (~₹1.7 lakh cr YTD via SIP) covering ~70-90% of FII selling depending on day; gap WIDENING. FPI ownership of Indian equities at ~16%, lowest in nearly two decades. **STRUCTURAL CRACK POINT: if FII outflows accelerate past ₹2.5 lakh cr by end-Q2, DII channel cracks.** Sonal Varma (Nomura): 'Don't expect any sustained pick-up in portfolio flows, as structural concerns over AI-related disruption to India's software industry remain'; recent RBI restrictions on AD forex hedging have increased FX hedge costs, deterring portfolio inflows; sees 'further scope for the rupee to depreciate.'

current.ytd_cy26_fii_outflow: ₹2 lakh cr (first 5 months of 2026 surpasses all of 2025's ₹1.66 lakh cr)
current.fpi_ownership: ~16% (lowest in 15-20 years AND below DII for first time ever)

active_triggers.condition: FII YTD CY26 outflow exceeds ₹2.5 lakh cr by end-Q2 (by 2026-06-30) — DII channel cracks
FII flow turn POSITIVE for 5 consecutive sessions — confirms India deployment-zone re-entry

top_edges.note: bank-nifty is sub-index inside nifty-50 node; edge captures specific bank-financials transmission

recent_signals.headline: **FII OUTFLOWS YTD ₹2.2 TRILLION — ALREADY EXCEED ENTIRE 2025 (₹1.66 trillion); DII OWNERSHIP NOW SURPASSES FII (20.9% vs 17.1% IN NIFTY 500).** Through first week of May, FPIs withdrew ₹1.92 lakh crore from Indian equities (Republic World); April alone saw ₹60,847 crore outflow. **DII ownership in Nifty 500 now 20.9%, surpassing FII's 17.1% — structural rotation, not just flow.** The DII absorption of 'nearly 90% of outflows through strong SIP-led buying' is the technical reason Indian indices haven't capitulated despite the structural bear. **The Sensex/Nifty +0.4%/+0.3% relief rally is misdirection** — IT exporter names (TCS, Infosys, HCL +3-4.5% on weaker rupee) catch the postponement-relief while the underlying economy worsens. **None of the three Indian deployment conditions (Brent <$95, FII flows net positive, INR retracement to ₹93) is met. All three moving the wrong direction.**
sources: Republic World (FII outflow ₹1.92 lakh crore through first week May)
Intel dossier 2026-05-19 A13; Markets dossier C6
recent_signals.verification: FII outflows YTD ₹2.2 trillion = CONFIRMED (Republic World + Business Standard); DII >FII in Nifty 500 = CONFIRMED structural reading; SIP-led DII absorption ~90% of outflows = REPORTED ongoing structural pattern.

recent_signals.headline: FII SINGLE-DAY NET SELL ₹8,437.56 cr MAY 11 = highest since April 24. DII +₹5,939.65 cr absorbed ~70% (₹5,939.65 cr). YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 LAKH CR — exceeds full-year 2025 (₹1.66 lakh cr) by ₹40k cr; added ₹14k cr in just 2 sessions May 11-12. Drivers: Trump rejection 'TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE' + Brent $105.58 + INR ₹95.54 fresh record breaking RBI ₹95.50 threshold + WH Sit-Room weighing renewed strikes. Cascade self-reinforcing: weaker rupee → imported inflation → bond yields up → debt-FPI out → more INR pressure. RBI forex reserves -$7.79bn week ended May 1 to $690.69bn = evidence of active intervention not stopping the move. Sonal Varma (Nomura) flagging 'further depreciation' on AI-disruption + RBI hedging restrictions deterring inflows.
sources: Business Standard May 11 + May 12 live blogs
Republic World / Outlook Money (₹2.06 lakh cr tally)
recent_signals.verification: FII -₹8,437.56 cr May 11 = CONFIRMED (NSDL primary); DII +₹5,939.65 cr = CONFIRMED (NSDL primary); YTD ₹2.06 lakh cr = CONFIRMED multi-source (Business Standard + Outlook Money); RBI reserves -$7.79bn = CONFIRMED (RBI primary)
